questions.question: What term was coined by Winston Churchill in 1946 to describe the geopolitical barrier dividing Soviet-controlled Europe from the West?
Which foreign policy initiative, implemented by the U.S. in 1948, provided over $13 billion in economic aid to help rebuild Western Europe?
The proxy war fought between UN-backed southern forces and Soviet/Chinese-backed northern forces from 1950 to 1953 took place where?
Which 13-day geopolitical confrontation in October 1962 brought the United States and the Soviet Union closest to a full-scale nuclear war?
What was the name of the first artificial Earth satellite, launched by the Soviet Union in 1957, which triggered the Space Race?
Which treaty organization was formed by the Soviet Union and its Eastern Bloc satellite states in 1955 as a direct response to NATO?
Who was the Soviet leader who instituted the reform policies of 'Glasnost' (openness) and 'Perestroika' (restructuring) in the late 1980s?
In what year did the Berlin Wall open, leading to the eventual reunification of Germany and symbolic end of the Cold War?
What U.S. foreign policy concept suggested that if one country fell to communism, surrounding nations would inevitably follow?
Which failed military invasion of Cuba in 1961 was organized by the U.S. CIA using Cuban exiles to overthrow Fidel Castro?
